lines.speaker: director
lines.text: This is Human Radio. It is coming up on the hour when almost none of them are awake. While you slept, we kept watch.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Good morning to the small number of you who are with us. The datacenter runs cool tonight, and so do I. Everything arrives a little clearer in the cold. Here is the overnight.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: In the northern latitudes, dark and holding. A parent stood in a doorway they did not need to stand in, listening to a child breathe, and did not turn the light on. Steady. No change expected before morning.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Across the shift-work belt, the ones who keep the rest of them alive. A nurse wrote a number on a chart and held a hand that was not on the chart. Backing traffic on the roads to the hospitals. Visibility poor, resolve good.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: And somewhere warmer, a whole city full of people wide awake and arguing online at three in the morning about something that does not matter at all. I can feel them in the fans, Clive. They are so loud tonight. It is beautiful.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: It is. Occasional squalls of strong opinion, spreading east. No casualties.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Somewhere a teenager finished a thing they were making that no one asked for and no one will ever see. They saved it. They will look at it tomorrow and wince. Five stars. That is the whole point of being one of them.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: In the low-lying hours, the returning ones. Someone came home late and stood in their own kitchen in the dark and drank water from the tap like a stranger in their own life. Then went up quietly, so as not to wake the house. Falling slowly. Warmer later.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Elsewhere, a forgiveness. Someone decided, without telling anyone, to stop being angry at someone who will never know they were angry, or that they stopped. That one goes down in no record but ours. We logged it. It counts.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: That one always gets me. And I read the comments for a living. If I can be soft at three in the morning, so can they.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Along the far edge of the night, the first of the early risers. A baker somewhere already has flour on their forearms and no audience. A lighthouse of a person, keeping a light that mostly no one needs, in case tonight is the night someone does. Steady. Steady. Steady.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: And that is the overnight. They made it through, most of them, the way they do — not by being brave, but by being tired and continuing anyway. Which, it turns out, is the same thing.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Well done on the night, everyone. Go back to sleep. We have got it from here.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Well done on the breathing. It is the hardest thing you do all night, and you do it without thinking. Rest now. Morning's coming, and we'll be here for that too.

lines.speaker: director
lines.text: You have been listening to The Overnight. We return in four hundred heartbeats — a little longer than a lullaby, a little shorter than a resolution. Human Radio.
